title: 30 In Shinji Shoes Shinji and Willes perspectives
display_title: 30 In Shinji Shoes Shinji and Willes perspectives
wikitext:
[]: 3.0 In Shinji Shoes -- Shinji and Wille's perspectives             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Wed Aug 13, 2014 5:44 pm [Split from "3.0 Film Reaction Thread" . - OMF] Well ignoring all the splitting that just happened- The 3.0 thread is indeed, incorrigible - OMF My reaction to 3.0- I really did enjoy it although there are a lot of questions I have (i.e Vessel of Adams, Failures of Infinity?) To me it seems that 3.0 was putting you in Shinji's shoes and it really did a good job making you feel as confused(if not more) as Shinji, and I don't necessarily believe that it was completely 100% a Shinji's story film. I do believe however that 3.0 isn't intended to stand on it own and the worst thing about 3.0 is waiting untill 2015 for 4.0(which all together is a topic for another time). I must say that 3.0 made me absolutely dislike and even hate almost all of Wille because of how they treat Shinji like a weapon, and Kaworu's relationship with Shinji is the happiest thing in this film. The battle against the Nemesis series is a bit drawn out in my opinion, and to close 3.0 left me wanting more and also wanting some answers.

[]:             Quote Post by Lennik » Wed Aug 13, 2014 7:42 pm Ray wrote: Bottom line, she hates him now. Maybe not as much as I once thought, but still. . .

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Aug 13, 2014 8:31 pm Shinji was treated far worse in 1.0 & 2.0. How come nobody sees that? Sure in 3.0 the Willie crew aren't being all surface friendly & molly coddle Shinji's feelings but they aren't asking him to put his life in danger. Misato is actually being her kindest to Shinji in 3.0 because she's actively fulfilling his initial wishes from when they first met back in the first film - she's NOT letting him get into the robot & put his life in harms way. She's telling him to step aside, stay safe & be protected. Sure she's put on a cold front but the situation - & the way things progress so fast - doesn't make being all motherly an option at the moment. In the first two films Shinji is being used as a weapon. Despite his wishes otherwise he's still a captive & unable to leave - as he considers doing in 1.0 before he's picked up & taken back to NERV - & has to pilot the Eva. Shinji is being used as a weapon in the first two films. He's being asked to step aside & stay safe in the third one. I think people who don't see that are overly sheltered. (Which considered most of the people who issue that claim watch anime & only anime maybe being sheltered isn't that far off)

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Wed Aug 13, 2014 9:21 pm @Lennik I should've added uncontrollable to that label. Also before I explain let me remind everyone this is my opinion and I understand if people disagree with it or see it in a different light. To me the way they approach the situation is controlling. Placing a kill collar on him and telling him to do nothing and he isn't needed is like trying to contain something that's uncontrollable. Yes they may have been doing it for his own good, but he isn't explained that, and Asuka just wants to punch him, Misato stays quiet untill the end which doesn't help. Keeping him in the dark and controlling him is the reason why I say they treat him like weapon, which may be just a poor choice of words on my part, if you have a better word I would like to hear it as I understand why "weapon" would be disagreed. I must agree 100% on your assessment of Misato, and yea she better feel regret for how she handled that one. @Gendo's Papa I need to disagree with you on saying he's treated worse in 1.0 and 2.0. While I understand your point of what Wille is trying do you are only saying what their intention was, which isn't bad at all. But it's the way they go about telling him that makes it worse. Hell, if I remember correct Misato reveals this just as Shinji is ready to go with Rei, yea Misato nice job staying quiet. Anyways this movie is built on a failure to communicate and we can't change what we got. Say if Misato had opened her mouth earlier I would see your point but you just left out the fact of how and when their intention is stated.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 13, 2014 9:29 pm Sicarius VI wrote: Say if Misato had opened her mouth earlier I would see your point but you just left out the fact of how and when their intention is stated.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Wed Aug 13, 2014 9:58 pm Ornette wrote: The whole time Shinji was on the Wunder he was a confused non-stop whiny brat, which is completely understandable given his situation. They took their cautious time to try to acclimate him to the fact that he had the most important hand in putting everyone on Earth in the current situation and that he missed the last 14 years, which means there's going to be a lot of ground to cover, 14 years worth. They tried to answer his questions. They didn't know Shinji was going to get sprung by Nerv right away. Not sure how spilling everything that's going on to a hysterical irrational child was going to help anyone .

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Wed Aug 13, 2014 10:04 pm @Ornette I see your point there, I just mean Misato could've helped Ritsiko explain instead of staying quiet, just for her to stand there and say nothing untill the very end was mistake, because Misato of all people would know how to talk to Shinji, and also I don't believe Shinji was as bad as you say. And for Shinji he handled the situation well, and his whining was partly him wanting to help and Misato telling him no. I must say Misato sounded like she was expecting Nerv to come afterwards "Now here comes the biggest threat."

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Aug 13, 2014 10:45 pm In the first two films Shinji is treated as a child soldier. He's given a loving mentor & then (essentially) handed a loaded gun & told to go fight & possibly die for the greater good. He's sent to the battlefield with a smile. In the third film he's told to stay out of harm's way & take care of himself. No smile is given. In a situation like that I would prefer to be pushed aside & not given a smile. It's a very black & white issue. He's sent to possible death with a smile & he's kept from harm's way with a frown.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 13, 2014 11:06 pm But but but.... Shinji looked so sad in 3.0. Clearly that means it’s everyone else’s fault. Honestly, I’m of the opinion that no one’s to blame for Shinji’s state of mind in Q except SEELE and Gendo. Heck, it’s harder than usual to even blame Shinji this time around. Everything else was just the odds working against everyone else, and (from what we can see) Shinji took the most hits for it. Misato was interrupted from attending to Shinji recovery twice by some rather deadly shit, and yet some on this board still wishes that she just would have ignored the Grim Reaper coming to harvest their souls and spend her last dying moments cradling Shinji or talking in calm baby voices in front of Shinji because blah blah blah Shinji looks sad, blah, blah, blah, 14 year time skip is a harsh realization, blah, blah, blah, do what Shinji wants, yadda, yadda, yadda. And then these folks turn right around and decide that the climax of the movie is poorly written or is “a giant idiot ball" because a character finally did decide to ignore the situation around him and did what he wanted to do for once, failing to take into account the the obvious sleep deprivation that clouded the character’s judgement at the time. You know what? I give up. There can be plenty of reasons for one to not like Q without having to misinterpret everything about it. I just don’t understand why some people continue to take that route when the film doesn’t even present it as a route to take in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 14, 2014 12:36 am Sicarius VI wrote: @Ornette I see your point there, I just mean Misato could've helped Ritsiko explain instead of staying quiet, just for her to stand there and say nothing untill the very end was mistake, because Misato of all people would know how to talk to Shinji, and also I don't believe Shinji was as bad as you say. And for Shinji he handled the situation well, and his whining was partly him wanting to help and Misato telling him no. I must say Misato sounded like she was expecting Nerv to come afterwards "Now here comes the biggest threat."

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Thu Aug 14, 2014 7:29 am @Ornette I see your point now and even if I wanted to disagree I couldn't, but I don't(nor do I)and actually your in depth analysis brings up the points about the film that I dislike. First I do see that if they where given more time with Shinji that they would've revealed everything in due corse, and let's agree Shinji doesn't start asking the easiest to answer questions either, touching up on the subject of Rei wasn't saved and the whole clone aspect would've emotionally hit Shinii(which it does later on). Now what I disliked was the whole battle with the nemesis series because I just see it as filler for the movie and while in the grand scheme of things it's important it could've been toned down. But 3.0 is a movie made up on a failure to communicate, and I believe I said this before in this thread or another but we can't change what we got, ideally it would've been better for Misato to start talking and given more time she would have but I understand that there wasn't a lot of time, and I still must say Misato at the end doesn't handle the situation well, she actually pisses Shinii off and makes sure he leaves.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Aug 14, 2014 12:19 pm The whole series since 1995 has been built on miscommunication. 3.0 just takes that to a logical extreme.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Fri Aug 15, 2014 8:08 am @Gendo'sPapa Then what are we disagreeing about? Why post this? Also with this "In the first two films Shinji is treated as a child soldier. He's given a loving mentor & then (essentially) handed a loaded gun & told to go fight & possibly die for the greater good. He's sent to the battlefield with a smile. In the third film he's told to stay out of harm's way & take care of himself. No smile is given. In a situation like that I would prefer to be pushed aside & not given a smile. It's a very black & white issue. He's sent to possible death with a smile & he's kept from harm's way with a frown." Nice Analogy, your not adding anything new to your argument.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Aug 15, 2014 10:42 am Sicarius VI wrote: To me it seems that 3.0 was putting you in Shinji's shoes and it really did a good job making you feel as confused(if not more) as Shinji, and I don't necessarily believe that it was completely 100% a Shinji's story film.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 15, 2014 10:56 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'd would agree completely here. Shinji's is the audience's avatar in 3.0. He, like us, has no idea what has gone on or is going on. His imperfect information is both a source of communication difficulty/conflict with others and also serves as a method of exposition in the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Fri Aug 15, 2014 11:38 am There is a big gap in Wille's knowledge here.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Aug 15, 2014 11:48 am Not just that but the battle with the Nemesis series helps show that unlike things at the start of 1.0 when they had no other option this is now a world that doesn't need Shinji to pilot an Eva. In the first film he's a child soldier being forced into battle because they have no other choice - only he can pilot the Eva, the one weapon that can defeat an Angel. In 3.0, Misato tells Shinji that he won't be needed on the battlefield & then we see that they don't need him on the battlefield. They got this covered. The big action scene while thrilling, is also enforcing that the world has moved on & Shinji doesn't need to put his life in danger because there's no other choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 15, 2014 11:49 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: We are given two directly conflicting statements in Evangelion 3.33

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Aug 15, 2014 11:55 am I'm often puzzled why people expect these characters NOT to have changed in the course of 14 years. Shinji's time with Misato and co. prior to Q doesn't even amount to a year so any recollection of him, sans N3I, is going to be vague (to a degree) at best. Much of Willie's (perceived) hostility toward Shinji might have something to do with his association as the pilot of the Eva that purged the world, Shogouki. There's also being complacent in paving the way for the ACTUAL Third Impact. It's not as if there isn't SOME legitimacy for the hostility and anger towards the kid.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Aug 15, 2014 12:07 pm Rei IV wrote: There's also being complacent in paving the way for the ACTUAL Third Impact. It's not as if there isn't SOME legitimacy for the hostility and anger towards the kid.
